feed.title: Dr. Pritam Deka — News & Weekly AI Digest

entry.title: Unified Multimodal Reasoning Emerges as Key to Trustworthy AI Systems Development
entry.summary: The strongest public signals in this briefing point less to a single breakthrough than to a shared engineering problem: turning model capability into systems that can be evaluated, trusted, and operated under real constr...

entry.title: Reliable AI Systems Need Grounding in Real World Evaluations and Evidence
entry.summary: The strongest public signals in this briefing point less to a single breakthrough than to a shared engineering problem: turning model capability into systems that can be evaluated, trusted, and operated under real constr...

entry.title: Agent Memory and Red Teaming Are Becoming Operational AI Tests
entry.summary: Agent memory, red teaming, serving releases, and attribution work are converging on a practical question: how do AI teams prove that agent behavior remains bounded, inspectable, and safe under operational pressure?...

entry.title: Guardrails and Review Agents Are Exposing AI Reliability Debt
entry.summary: Guardrails, review agents, multimodal search, and serving releases point to the same bottleneck: capability is less useful when teams cannot verify what changed, why it worked, and where it may fail...

entry.title: Evidence Is Becoming the Real Interface for AI Systems
entry.summary: The most interesting work in agents, multimodal RAG and document intelligence is converging on one problem: models must show where an answer came from, how it was assembled and where it can still fail...
